Video.summary: World record fly fisherman Nathaniel Linville shares his journey from troubled youth and addiction to redemption through fly fishing. After hitting rock bottom, he finds purpose in sobriety and relentless dedication, ultimately rising to the top as a world-record permit angler in the Florida Keys.
Video.title: Life on the Line

Video.summary: In part 1 of the British episodes, Andy starts his road trip around the United Kingdom in the Cotswolds where he sets out to hunt and taste the beautiful venison that Michelin star chef, Mark Robinson has to offer. He then proceeds to travel to the border of Scotland where he samples some of the finest and most historic bird shooting practices found throughout the British Isles.
Video.title: Wildbound - "UK Part 1"

Video.summary: Bassmaster legend Mike Iaconelli joins Nick in a wild time chasing down striped bass blitzes off the Jersey Coast, getting bent rods from the boat and surf.
Video.title: "Ike on Stripers"

Video.summary: C.A. joins Rhett Morris in Charlotte Harbor for an inshore slam adventure in one of Florida’s most productive estuaries.
Video.title: "Harbor Master"

Video.summary: CA and Marlin head to the beautiful waters of Tarpon Springs for cobia and trout.
Video.title: "Cobia Co-Op"

Video.summary: Heroes are honored and embark on the hunt of a lifetime at the Bucks for the Brave event hosted by Record Rack and Trinity Oaks Non-Profit.
Video.title: "Bucks for the Brave"

Video.summary: CA and Trevor field test some new Flats Class Exclusive Rods.
Video.title: "Two-Timing Trevor"

Video.summary: CA heads to the northern regions of the Nature Coast for a multi-species day.
Video.title: "Pressure Makes Diamonds"

Video.summary: This episode we make our way through South Carolina, where we stop in Clemson, Travelers Rest and Charleston!!
Video.title: "Lowcountry Buzzer Beater! (Redfish on Fly)"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor catch two thirds of a slam when they bring Tom’s new Yellowfin 24’ Bay up to Key Largo for some exciting flats fishing.
Video.title: "Two Out Of Three Ain't Bad"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor are joined by Captain Mason Baker to sample some early morning Islamorada backcountry baby tarpon action.
Video.title: "Baby Tarpon With Mason Baker"

Video.summary: After a successful morning of baby tarpon fishing, Captains Tom Rowland, Rich Tudor and Mason Baker slide over to the legendary downtown Islamorada flats to check on the resurgence of Florida Keys bonefish.
Video.title: "Islamorada Bonefish With Mason Baker"

Video.summary: Captains Tom Rowland and Rich Tudor head into the Everglades backcountry out of Key Largo to fish the shallow grass flats for tailing redfish.
Video.title: "Everglades Redfish Out Of Key Largo"

Video.summary: Michael and Larken Letchworth set out on a new adventure, exploring food fishing and the great outdoors. This father son duo are making memories that will last a lifetime and will forever be etched in the digital world.
Video.title: "The Beginning"

Video.summary: With the drought ending and rain headed their way, the crew hunts the final days of the first split in Bayou Black with new friends Jesse Griffiths and Dirk Sorrells.
Video.title: "Bayou Black Belly Bands"

Video.summary: The second split is here, and the crew decides to try their luck at historic Old Hickory in Lacour, Louisiana. Ducks, deer, and mouth-watering meals are all on the menu.
Video.title: "Lacour Long Gravy"

Video.summary: Guadalajara’s heritage runs deep. On the DIRT crew’s latest adventure, they explored the region’s rich history from volcanic highlands to secret surf coves. Chefs Xrysw Ruelas and Óscar Segundo sent Josh and co. to load up on locally grown coffee, spearfish between catching waves, and follow freshly harvested agave on its journey to becoming tequila. After learning the ropes of Mexico’s national sport, Charreria, and hitting up a Lucha Libre match, all the local flavors came together in a feast backed by thousands of years of tradition. There really is something special in the soil here.
Video.title: DIRT - "Harvesting Tequila, Surfing Secret Coves & Tasting Legendary Mexican Flavors"

Image.alt: DIRT - "Harvesting Tequila, Surfing Secret Coves & Tasting Legendary Mexican Flavors"
DIRT - "Harvesting Tequila, Surfing Secret Coves & Tasting Legendary Mexican Flavors"
DIRT - "Harvesting Tequila, Surfing Secret Coves & Tasting Legendary Mexican Flavors"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger take Plantation Boat Mart’s new 36’ Invincible out to the swordfish grounds.
Video.title: "Swordfishing out of Plantation Boat Mart"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger break in their new Invincible Catamaran with a trip to fish out of Palm Beach with their friend Captain Dave Fields.
Video.title: "Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"

Image.alt: "Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"
"Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"
"Palm Beach in the new Invincible Catamaran"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger fly kites with Captain Dave Fields for a great day of sailfishing out of Sailfish Marina in Palm Beach.
Video.title: "Palm Beach Sailfishing"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger take a long run out into the Gulf from Key West to sample the bottom fishing and the long range capability of their new Invincible Catamaran.
Video.title: "Way Out in the Gulf Part 1"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger continue fishing after taking a long run out into the Gulf from Key West to sample the bottom fishing and the long range capability of their new Invincible Catamaran.
Video.title: "Way Out in the Gulf Part 2"

Video.summary: Captains Scott Walker and Steve Rodger fish out of Key West for tunas and sailfish while battling big seas and big sharks.
Video.title: "Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"

Image.alt: "Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"
"Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"
"Chasing Pelagics in Big Seas Out of Key West"

Video.summary: In Stomping Grounds Episode 5, our crew follows the road all the way down to where it ends on the tropical sun-baked island of Key West at the southern tip of the Florida Keys. Surrounded by water, Key West is a boater's paradise with historic seaports and marinas offering a bustling waterfront with historic schooners, dive boats, sport fishing charters, parasailing adventures and scenic coastal excursions. Host Ryan McVinney hops aboard a custom hybrid diesel-electric power catamaran with solar panels, called "Squid" for an in-depth eco-tour of Key West National Wildlife Refuge, including snorkeling in a sponge garden with tropical fish. Next a quick stop in Dry Tortugas National Park via the 110-foot Gladding Hearn power catamaran the "Yankee Freedom III" to visit historic Fort Jefferson, then we jump on a 2020 Sea Hunt 255 Ultra for a ride through the dolphin playground to a secret local boating spot known as Snipe Keys with pristine white sandbars. Back in town we we board the Nau Santa Maria - a replica of Christopher Columbus' sailboat. Finally we head 50 miles offshore to ply the wakes of the shrimp boats with fishing charter captain Chris Trosset aboard his Contender 35ST center console boat.
Video.title: "Key West - A Boater's Paradise"

Video.summary: In this special 20th-anniversary episode, "Deer & Deer Hunting TV" takes a nostalgic journey through two decades of hunting television. Hosts Dan Schmidt and Brad Rucks lead viewers through memorable moments.
Video.title: "Twenty Years"

Video.summary: Dan Schmidt and Mark Kayser demonstrate the strategic use of terrain and natural cover to outsmart big whitetails. Then Mark heads to fill his tag in the rugged landscapes of South Dakota.
Video.title: "Advanced Techniques for Hunting Bog Whitetails"

Video.summary: We explore the sophisticated world of the whitetail deer's senses, exploring how these remarkable creatures have evolved to pattern hunters through their keen sense of smell and heightened sensory awareness.
Video.title: "Patterning Hunters"

Video.summary: WWE pro wrestling hall of fame legend Jake the Snake Roberts joins Nick in a no holds barred main event match against Jersey stripers during an incredible Raritan Bay run. Nick and Jake put the finishing moves down on bass, then Nick finds a monster bass bite on topwater poppers later in the day with good friends Bender and Drew Dog.
Video.title: "Jake The Snake Vs. Striped Bass"

Video.summary: Surf Fishing for Stripers is at full on Fall Run intensity in New Jersey. Nick assembles a hardcore local crew of surfcasters including rock star Dean Ween and Man vs Food host Casey Webb to for mindblowing surf action from the sands as they come tight with hundreds of stripers in an all out blitz.
Video.title: "Surf Striper"

Video.summary: Rock star Dean Ween joins Nick for splashing, crashing and thrashing surf action as the Jersey surf is firing for striped bass blitzing on bait.
Video.title: "Surf Striped Bass"

Video.summary: While releasing a defeated tarpon healthy after an epic battle is the ultimate victory, everything else is measured in heartbreak and degrees of failure. For Justin, putting Ethan on his first silver king requires patience and a tasty diversion by a local chef.
Video.title: "Silver King Thing"

Video.summary: Meredith learns the value of the Trash Can Slam on a flounder trip with - Capt. Justin that turns into a multi-species challenge.”
Video.title: "Flounder To Slam"

Video.summary: Capt Justin Leake and Meredith McCord divert attention from what’s not happening with the tarpon bite to using CoastMaster, light tackle and experience to find snapper success on heavily fished public spots.
Video.title: "Dog Daze Delight"

Video.summary: When the Bay Point Billfish Open returns, we hop on board the Ellie B for some offshore competition and follow the event’s epic dockside party.
Video.title: "Billfish Open Rebirth"

Video.summary: About 2-3 months ago Mark purchased a 300+ acre farm in Kentucky. After running trail cameras and putting food plots on the farm, Mark now has the chance to get in the stand on opening day of Kentucky's bow season. There have been plenty of shooter bucks on the farm but only a few still are holding their velvet. Mark has his sights set on a Giant Kentucky Velvet Whitetail. The day before opening day Salt River puts on a celebration that features the @theswonbrothers.
Video.title: "Giant Kentucky Velvet Whitetails"

Video.summary: It's 2016 and Mark heads down to Kansas for an early season Muzzleloader Whitetail hunt with Salt River Outfitters. It was hot in the stand with little deer movement until the moon phase switched and - one of the bucks Mark had been looking for stepped out. Kansas is one of those states that has an early Muzzleloader season that opens in September, and if you can get the weather and the moon to align you can catch a big deer on his feet in daylight.
Video.title: "Giant 15 Point Kansas Whitetail, Early Season Hunt"

Image.alt: "Giant 15 Point Kansas Whitetail, Early Season Hunt"
"Giant 15 Point Kansas Whitetail, Early Season Hunt"
"Giant 15 Point Kansas Whitetail, Early Season Hunt"

Video.summary: Mark is still on the South Island of New Zealand chasing the Wingshooting Slam. It’s Mark's last day to complete the waterfowl portion of the slam and he still needs a Black Swan and a Shoveler. This hunt is taking place on a freshwater lake right off the ocean. The temperature is unusually warm for this time of year, but thankfully that hasn’t affected the numbers of birds around.
Video.title: "Hunting Black Swan and Shoveler: New Zealand | The Journey WIthin"

Image.alt: "Hunting Black Swan and Shoveler: New Zealand | The Journey WIthin"
"Hunting Black Swan and Shoveler: New Zealand | The Journey WIthin"
"Hunting Black Swan and Shoveler: New Zealand | The Journey WIthin"

Video.summary: On this episode Mark leaves the peach state of Georgia to travel across the state line into Florida. Mark Peterson is on the quest to harvest Wood Duck, Black Bellied Whistling Duck, Blue Winged Teal, and a Mottled Duck. Florida is one of two places that the Mottled Duck calls home. The wood duck one of the most colorful ducks of the 43 waterfowl slam species sure poses to be a colorful drone retrieval to say the least.
Video.title: "Duck Hunting Florida - Wood Duck, Mottled Duck and Much More | - Waterfowl Slam"

Image.alt: "Duck Hunting Florida - Wood Duck, Mottled Duck and Much More | - Waterfowl Slam"
"Duck Hunting Florida - Wood Duck, Mottled Duck and Much More | - Waterfowl Slam"
"Duck Hunting Florida - Wood Duck, Mottled Duck and Much More | - Waterfowl Slam"

Video.summary: Three friends finally pack their bags and head west for the annual HuntStand pronghorn hunting camp—each with a unique goal in mind.
Video.title: "Camp Cutters"

Video.summary: Notoriously nocturnal and living in some of the densest cover imaginable, the black-tailed deer is generally known to be more ghostly than the most challenging old whitetails.
Video.title: "From The Fog"

Video.summary: Texas isn’t all mesquite flats, corn feeders, and wire fences. Some of the most extreme terrain in the country can be found in the Lone Star State, and it just so happens that hunting opportunities abound for the adventurous.
Video.title: "West Texas Sherpa"

Video.summary: Will Cooper finally trades 3 years of tough times for triumph in his quest to tag an out-of-state buck with a bow.
Video.title: "Nine Lives"

Video.summary: Capt. Jared Raskob joins his long time friends Capt. Tyler Webb and Capt. Casey Yaworski on a hunt for Sails in Islamorada, Florida.
Video.title: "Tyler & Casey"

Video.summary: This week we're joined by our good friend and Worship Leader, Rob Key. A tremendous musical talent that's chased the country music dream, but found his calling as worship leader at Taylorsville Community Church. Rob's love for serving the Lord is his top priority, but he certainly enjoys the great outdoors and especially doing a little fishing.
Video.title: "Worship, Music, and Bassin'"

Video.summary: LBP Spotlights Internet Personality and YouTube Star Lawson Lindsey as he searches for trophy fish at the Headwaters of the Loxahatchee River.
Video.title: "Lawson Lindsey"

Video.summary: On this episode of Huckberry Homes, we caught our good friend and photographer James Barkman out in the wild. He gave us a tour of his vintage "Yellow Submarine" inspired VW Van and broke down some of the modifications he has made to make this adventure bus feel more like home.
Video.title: "Full Vintage Van Tour | Inside This Photographer's Ultimate Camper Van | James Barkman"

Image.alt: "Full Vintage Van Tour | Inside This Photographer's Ultimate Camper Van | James Barkman"
"Full Vintage Van Tour | Inside This Photographer's Ultimate Camper Van | James Barkman"
"Full Vintage Van Tour | Inside This Photographer's Ultimate Camper Van | James Barkman"

Video.summary: Hunting off the northeastern coast of Australia on Marble island could be one of the most beautiful sceneries in the world. Mark Peterson takes the 38 hour trip to Australia only to arrive in camp and have enough time to change and hit the ocean coastline looking for Axis & Javan Rusa deer.
Video.title: "Hunting Marble Island for Axis and Rusa Deer"

Image.alt: "Hunting Marble Island for Axis and Rusa Deer"
"Hunting Marble Island for Axis and Rusa Deer"
"Hunting Marble Island for Axis and Rusa Deer"

Video.summary: Mark Peterson is in Hungary with his dad Earl going after Red Stag and Mouflon. Every once in a while, you have one of those trips where it seems that all odds are against you. Mark couldn't fly in until a day late, so he only had one day to hunt. Earl's luggage, along with his weapon and the field producers' cameras didn't show up but they didn't let that stand in their way. With a borrowed gun and a couple of iPhones, they started hunting.
Video.title: "Giant Red Stag in Hungary"

Video.summary: Mark Peterson is still in Veracruz, Mexico chasing after a free-range Axis Deer. With warmer temps still presenting challenges, Mark and his guide head to a Michon overlooking a pond full of crocodiles. With the temps getting as warm as 90 degrees, the Axis deer search for water to stay hydrated. With time running out on the final day, they catch movement in the brush and a beautiful Axis buck steps out to take a drink.
Video.title: "North America Deer Slam Axis Deer"
